response:
**Bottom line: pilot, not full-grid-always — but scoped and coded, not the raw either/or from the prompt.**

The panel didn't split cleanly on "run everything" vs "pilot the expensive arm" — all three advisors converged on a third option once pressed:

- **process-economist**: pilot-first is real savings, not deferred cost, because the decision rule only needs the Tiered-vs-Sonnet-solo pair.
- **lean-process-engineer**: agreed in principle, but flagged that "pilot the most expensive arm" as originally framed was underspecified (no pilot size/selection) — reuse ADR 0019's *already*-precise per-eval escalation mechanism instead of inventing a new one.
- **session-operator**: rejected letting a live agent judge "looks decidable" (optional-stopping/cheap-path bias) and flagged that unconditionally dropping the Haiku-solo/Opus-solo arms would silently cut the pre-registration's required "Opus-quality at sub-Opus-cost" report.

**Decision (ADR 0035, accepted):** sequence the two decision-gating arms (Tiered, Sonnet-solo) first across all 8 pre-screened tasks; reuse ADR 0019's per-eval sequential-replicate escalation unmodified (no new task-cutting); add one small coded gate script — not a live agent call — that stops the gating pair's spend once both axes (quality-delta CI and cost ratio, each requiring ≥2 reps before gating) are unambiguous. Haiku-solo/Opus-solo still run their own full grid regardless, since they're a standing report requirement, not a decision input.

The independent verifier reproduced all 6 load-bearing citations against source (one correction folded in: a coded escalation *predicate* already exists elsewhere in the repo, but not a sequential-*stop* controller, which is the actual gap this ADR fills). The file is at `docs/decisions/0035-issue-41-execution-sequencing.md`, passes `adr-lint`, and is uncommitted pending your review.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0035-issue-41-execution-sequencing.md ---
---
id: 0035
title: "Issue #41 tiered-agent test: sequence the gating pair, coded stop, not full-grid-always"
status: accepted
summary: "Run the two gating arms (Tiered, Sonnet-solo) first across all 8 pre-screened tasks; reuse ADR 0019's per-eval escalation unmodified; add one small coded gate script (tested) that stops the gating pair's spend once both axes (quality delta, cost ratio) are unambiguous. Haiku-solo/Opus-solo run their own full grid regardless — a standing report, not a decision input. Rejects both full-grid-always and an ad hoc reduced-task pilot."
---

# 0035 — issue #41 execution sequencing: gated pilot, not full-grid-always

- Date: 2026-07-12
- Owner: PM
- Panel: opposing counsel (process-economist spend-first vs lean-process-engineer rigor-first) +
  session-operator (execution realism); verifier reproduced all 6 claims (1 correction below).
- Context: Comment 1's pre-registration (`ISSUE-41.md:25-59`) fixes arms/tasks/reps/decision
  rule but is silent on execution order or whether the full grid runs unconditionally. Comment 3
  estimates ~250-300 invocations (`ISSUE-41.md:71`) for the full 4x8x3 grid. The decision rule
  (`ISSUE-41.md:51`) gates ONLY on Tiered vs Sonnet-solo (delta >= -0.05 AND tokens/wall-clock
  <= 0.7x); Haiku-solo/Opus-solo are floor/ceiling context arms, reported not gated on.

## Decision
1. **Pre-screen unchanged.** 1-rep Haiku-solo vs Opus-solo, drop saturated tasks (`ISSUE-41.md:42`).
2. **Sequence the gating pair first.** Tiered and Sonnet-solo — the only pair the decision rule
   compares (`ISSUE-41.md:51`) — run rep 1 across all 8 final tasks before the context arms.
3. **Reuse ADR 0019 item 2 unmodified; no new task-cutting.** Escalate a gating-pair cell to rep
   2/3 only where that eval's CI straddles the boundary (`0019-eval-verdict-validity.md:17`);
   never spend a rep on an unambiguous cell. No ad hoc "reduced pilot subset" — reusing the
   precise per-eval mechanism avoids an unspecified pilot size.
4. **One small coded gate (new, tested — not a live agent call).** Per escalation round, a
   script (shape of `eval_verdict.py`, tested like `eval_verdict_test.py`) computes (a)
   eval-clustered CI on delta = mean(Tiered)-mean(Sonnet-solo) vs -0.05, AND (b) token/wall-clock
   ratio vs 0.7x, each needing >=2 reps before that axis gates (rep-1 alone never decides —
   variance guard). STOP once both axes are unambiguous; else escalate per (3). Verified gap: no
   coded sequential-STOP controller exists yet (`0019:17`'s "no owned stopping controller" is
   prose); the sole existing coded escalation predicate
   (`2026-07-09-ep-remeasure-hermetic/aggregate.py:65-66`) is a different per-eval Option-E
   diagnostic — this is new, narrow code, not a duplicate.
5. **Context arms not gated by the early stop.** Haiku-solo/Opus-solo run their own full grid
   (3x8, ADR 0019 escalation applies within them) regardless of gating-pair speed — the
   Opus-quality report (`ISSUE-41.md:51`) is a standing deliverable, not a decision input.
6. **Scoping addendum, not a re-registration** — arms/tasks/reps/decision-rule unchanged; only
   order + coded stop are added, pre-committed before any invocation runs.

## Justification
Full-grid-always spends the full ~250-300 invocation budget (`ISSUE-41.md:71`) even when the
gating pair resolves at rep 1 — the exact waste ADR 0019 #2 and ADR 0027 #4 already forbid one
grain down (replicate/cell); this applies the same adopted mechanism one layer up (arm
sequencing) instead of inventing new machinery or letting a live agent eyeball "looks decidable"
(optional-stopping bias). Context arms stay unconditional so savings are real, not a narrowed
report.

## Assumptions
- [verified] decision rule gates only on Tiered vs Sonnet-solo; Opus-solo is "also report," not
  a gate — `ISSUE-41.md:51`.
- [verified] ADR 0019 #2's per-eval escalation + "no owned stopping controller" —
  `docs/decisions/0019-eval-verdict-validity.md:17`.
- [verified] ADR 0027 #4's "top-up only on straddling cells" —
  `docs/decisions/0027-ep-remeasure-arm-construction.md:19`.
- [verified, corrected] no coded sequential-STOP controller exists for #41 (prose only); the one
  coded escalation predicate is ADR 0027's Option-E per-eval diagnostic
  (`benchmarks/2026-07-09-ep-remeasure-hermetic/aggregate.py:65-66`) — a different mechanism;
  verifier corrected an initial overbroad "no coded predicate anywhere" claim.
- [checkable] the >=2-rep-before-cost-axis guard prevents a single Tiered iterate-cycle outlier
  flipping the 0.7x call — owner: script builder; test: synthetic 2-rep spread crossing 0.7x on
  rep 1 alone asserts the gate defers; result: pending (build after current batch, `ISSUE-41.md:21`).
- [unverifiable] WEAKEST: sequencing the gating pair first vs interleaving all 4 arms doesn't
  bias task-order effects (context/rate-limit drift) — REOPEN-IF deltas show a task-order trend on
  the full run; then randomize/interleave arm order.

## Rejected alternatives
- Full grid unconditionally — ignores ADR 0019/0027 precedent one grain down; full budget spend
  on a rep-1-decisive gating pair.
- Ad hoc reduced-task pilot — underspecified size/selection, duplicates the pre-screen
  (`ISSUE-41.md:42`); rejected for ADR 0019's full-8-task escalation.
- Live-agent "looks decidable" judgment — reintroduces optional-stopping/cheap-path bias;
  rejected for a coded, tested predicate.
- Drop Haiku-solo/Opus-solo once the gating pair resolves — silently cuts the pre-registered
  Opus-quality report (`ISSUE-41.md:51`).

## Revisit triggers
- Coded gate's cost-axis estimate swings past rep 2 under the guard -> raise the minimum reps
  before cost-axis evaluation.
- The run never triggers an early stop before the full 3x8 gating-pair grid completes -> ceremony
  with no realized savings; fold ordering back, drop the coded-gate script.
- A task-order trend appears (REOPEN-IF above) -> randomize/interleave arm order on re-run.
